title: HTML Comments Contain Sensitive Keywords
description: HTML comments containing sensitive keywords (passwords, API keys, TODO notes).
evidence: Sensitive keywords found in HTML comments.
riskImpact: HTML comments are visible to anyone who views page source.
explanation: Developers often leave comments referencing sensitive systems or credentials.
fixSteps: Remove all comments containing sensitive information.
Use a build step that strips HTML comments.
Move developer notes to server-side files.

codeExamples.label: Webpack
codeExamples.code: new TerserPlugin({ terserOptions: { output: { comments: false } }, extractComments: false })

title: Server header truncated
description: Server: gunicorn/19.9.0 (truncated) — the truncated string indicates a configured but possibly lying value.
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-server-header-truncated` on the response body.
riskImpact: Validate that your reverse proxy is not echoing real upstream version
explanation: Server: gunicorn/19.9.0 (truncated) — the truncated string indicates a configured but possibly lying value.
fixSteps: Validate that your reverse proxy is not echoing real upstream version

codeExamples.label: Next.js (next.config.mjs)
Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;

title: PHP session cookie naming exposes runtime
description: PHPSESSID in cookie name reveals PHP.
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-php-version-in-cookie` on the response body.
riskImpact: Rename the session cookie to a generic opaque value
explanation: PHPSESSID in cookie name reveals PHP.
fixSteps: Rename the session cookie to a generic opaque value

Nginx

explanation: Public config.js / settings.js often leaks API keys, environment hints, or feature flags.
fixSteps: Move secrets out of public config files

explanation: Sitemap.xml is publicly accessible by design. Make sure it doesn't reference private endpoints.

explanation: robots.txt disallows nothing — that's fine but worth noting.

riskImpact: Restrict ACAO to the customer-facing origin

fixSteps: Restrict ACAO to the customer-facing origin

Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;

title: reCAPTCHA site key exposure
description: Google reCAPTCHA site key is exposed in client-side code by design. Site keys are not secret.
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-recaptcha-key-leaked` on the response body.
riskImpact: This is informational; rotate site keys if you ever suspect abuse
explanation: Google reCAPTCHA site key is exposed in client-side code by design. Site keys are not secret.
fixSteps: This is informational; rotate site keys if you ever suspect abuse

codeExamples.label: Next.js (next.config.mjs)
Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;

title: Google Analytics tracking ID exposed
description: GA tracking ID (UA-XXXX / G-XXXX) is publicly visible. Not a security issue by itself.
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-ga-tracking-id-leaked` on the response body.
riskImpact: This is informational
explanation: GA tracking ID (UA-XXXX / G-XXXX) is publicly visible. Not a security issue by itself.
fixSteps: This is informational

codeExamples.label: Next.js (next.config.mjs)
Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;

title: nginx version disclosed in 404 / error pages
description: Default nginx error pages and the Server response header often expose the exact nginx version (e.g. 'nginx/1.18.0'). Knowing the version lets attackers map CVEs quickly.
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-nginx-version-404` on the response body.
riskImpact: Hide the nginx version in server_tokens off; remove or genericize the Server header.
explanation: Default nginx error pages and the Server response header often expose the exact nginx version (e.g. 'nginx/1.18.0'). Knowing the version lets attackers map CVEs quickly.
fixSteps: Add 'server_tokens off;' to nginx.conf.
Use a custom error page that does not include the server version.
Strip the Server header at the reverse proxy or CDN.

codeExamples.label: Next.js (next.config.mjs)
Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;

title: Apache version disclosed in 404 / error pages
description: Default Apache error pages expose the full server version, loaded modules, and OS in the footer (e.g. 'Apache/2.4.57 (CentOS) Server at example.com Port 443').
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-apache-version-404` on the response body.
riskImpact: Set ServerTokens Prod and ServerSignature Off in httpd.conf.
explanation: Default Apache error pages expose the full server version, loaded modules, and OS in the footer (e.g. 'Apache/2.4.57 (CentOS) Server at example.com Port 443').
fixSteps: Set 'ServerTokens Prod' to expose only 'Apache'.
Set 'ServerSignature Off' to remove the version footer.
Use a custom ErrorDocument for all 4xx/5xx codes.

codeExamples.label: Next.js (next.config.mjs)
Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;

title: IIS version disclosed in 404 / error pages
description: Internet Information Services (IIS) leaks its version in the Server header and in detailed error pages, including the .NET CLR version and machine name.
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-iis-version-404` on the response body.
riskImpact: Hide the IIS version via httpResponseHeader and disable detailed error pages.
explanation: Internet Information Services (IIS) leaks its version in the Server header and in detailed error pages, including the .NET CLR version and machine name.
fixSteps: Use URL Rewrite or web.config to remove the Server header.
Set httpErrors mode=Custom to suppress detailed errors.
Disable 'Send Detailed Error Messages' in IIS Manager.

codeExamples.label: Next.js (next.config.mjs)
Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;

title: Rails default error page disclosed
description: Rails in development mode renders the 'Welcome aboard' and detailed exception pages with source code excerpts, routes, and request parameters. In production, exceptions through ActionDispatch::DebugExceptions can still leak similar information if not configured.
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-rails-error` on the response body.
riskImpact: Run with RAILS_ENV=production and configure consider_all_requests_local=false.
explanation: Rails in development mode renders the 'Welcome aboard' and detailed exception pages with source code excerpts, routes, and request parameters. In production, exceptions through ActionDispatch::DebugExceptions can still leak similar information if not configured.
fixSteps: Set RAILS_ENV=production.
Set config.consider_all_requests_local = false in production.rb.
Configure config.action_dispatch.show_exceptions = :rescuable.

codeExamples.label: Next.js (next.config.mjs)
Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;

title: AWS S3 NoSuchBucket error pattern exposed
description: S3 bucket hosting exposes 'NoSuchBucket' or 'AccessDenied' XML error responses that reveal the bucket name, region, and AWS account ID via the x-amz-request-id header.
evidence: Information disclosure finding for `information-disclosure-s3-nosuchbucket` on the response body.
riskImpact: Genericize S3 error responses with a CDN/WAF and avoid linking directly to bucket hostnames.
explanation: S3 bucket hosting exposes 'NoSuchBucket' or 'AccessDenied' XML error responses that reveal the bucket name, region, and AWS account ID via the x-amz-request-id header.
fixSteps: Front S3 with CloudFront or another CDN that rewrites error pages.
Disable direct bucket hostname access via bucket policies.
Use a vanity domain for S3-backed static sites.

codeExamples.label: Next.js (next.config.mjs)
Nginx
codeExamples.code: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
